title: Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina
content:
{{Top}} {{Update|[[Han Solo & Chewbacca 1]], [[Han Solo & Chewbacca 7]], [[Han Solo & Chewbacca 8]], [[Han Solo & Chewbacca 10]], [[From a Certain Point of View]], [[The Sand Will Provide]], [[Star Wars (2015) 5|''Star Wars'' (2015) 5]], [[Doctor Aphra Annual 3|''Doctor Aphra Annual'' 3]], [[The Key to Remembering]], [[Chapter 10: The Passenger]], [[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2016)|Star Wars: Complete Locations]], [[Connections (Disney Gallery: The Mandalorian)|Connections]]}} {{Structure |image=[[File:ChalmunsCantina-TMCh5.png]] |name=Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina |constructed= |destroyed= |type=[[Cantina]]{{Film|IV}} |location=[[Wonto District]],''[[Star Wars Outlaws]]'' [[Mos Eisley]], [[Tatooine]] |interest=*[[Chalmun's office]][[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2016)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']] *[[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina hangar]] *Main lounge |rebuilt= |builder=Early Tatooine |height= |width= |affiliation=*[[Vriichon brothers|Vriichon Brothers]] *[[Chalmun]] *Another [[Unidentified boss|boss]]{{StoryCite|story=The Key to Remembering|book=From a Certain Point of View: Return of the Jedi}} }} {{Quote|Most of the best freighter pilots can be found here. Only watch your step. This place can be a little rough.|Obi-Wan Kenobi, to Luke Skywalker|}} '''Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina''', also known simply as '''Chalmun's Cantina''', or as the '''Mos Eisley Cantina''', was a [[Cantina|drinking and dining establishment]] located in the [[city]] of [[Mos Eisley]] on the [[desert]] [[Planet|world]] of [[Tatooine]]. Although a dimly lit tavern known for frequent outbreaks of violence, it was a popular stopping point for [[pilot]]s, [[smuggler]]s, [[bounty hunter]]s, [[alien]] misfits, and renegades. Also, [[droid]]s were not allowed inside. ==Description== [[File:FettCrashesCantina-2015StarWars5.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Boba addresses the patrons of the Mos Eisley Cantina]] Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina was a sandstone construct originally built as a fortress to defend against [[Tusken Raider]] raids. It was located on [[Inner Curved Street]] in the outskirts of the [[Mos Eisley]] [[spaceport]] on [[Tatooine]]. The building housed a bar area with a maximum occupancy of 102 patrons, with seats at the bar and at a variety of tables and booths. The heads of several creatures, including a [[crocodina]],{{RegalRobot|set=Regal Robot|text=''Elements Series''|pack=Crocodina Magnet|link=shop/star-wars/starwars-art-sculpture-decor/crocodina-magnet-exclusive|archivedate=20240723134254}} were mounted on the walls around the bar. The cantina's bar had twelve drinks available on tap, with the size of a standard drink being 16 ounces. The most popular drinks at the cantina were [[blue milk]], [[Tatooine Sunset]], [[Ardees|Jawa Juice]], [[Yatooni Boska]], [[Tatooni Junko]], and [[Hutt's Delight]]. There was also a secret drinks list that patrons could ask for, which included the items [[Red dwarf (alcohol)|Red Dwarf]], [[Cassandra Sunrise]], [[Nova Blaster]], and [[Flameout]].''[[Star Wars: Smuggler's Guide]]'' The [[Bith]] "Fiery" [[Figrin D'an]], [[kloo horn]] player and leader of the [[Figrin D'an and the Modal Nodes|Modal Nodes]], was a regular act at Chalmun's Cantina. Frequent clients were privy to a number of special accommodations that could be requested from the owners. If a patron were to ask for the Nova Blaster with Savareen Brandy swapped for Corellian, the patron would then be escorted into a hidden gun closet where they could purchase black market weapons. ===Service areas=== [[File:Myo_Muftak_Lirin_Djas.jpg|thumb|210x210px|Patrons on the main bar of Chalmun's Cantina]] Other than the public bar, the building housed a private bar, staffed by [[Nimbanel]] barman [[Husklf]] and reserved exclusively for the owner, [[Chalmun]] and the occasional celebrity or [[Hutt]] affiliate visitor. The [[Wookiee]] also kept an [[Chalmun's office|office]] behind the private bar, where he arranged business with the bartenders and observed the main lounge from behind a secret surveillance window. [[Chalmun's security chief]] used the back of the office to listen in to the patrons' private conversations for any signs of trouble. Aware of his clientele's nefarious activities, Chalmun turned one of the back rooms into an [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina hangar|open-air hangar]] where he stored [[Utility crane ship|his escape craft]], tended to by his [[Chalmun's personal pilot|personal pilot]]. On the cellar of the Cantina was a laboratory where [[Wuher]] prepared the special drinks that were then routed via a buried pipe into the bar dispensers. Behind the lab was a caustic pit where unwanted items (and occasionally bodies) were dumped, as well as ancient catacombs that predated the building's construction. ==History== ===Construction and original use=== The Cantina was originally constructed by early Tatooine pioneers as a fortification against Sand People raids. As Mos Eisley expanded, the building saw use as an [[armory]], a brewery and a flophouse for vagrants. Eventually, it was purchased by the [[Vriichon brothers]], who turned it into an illegal [[spice]] den. Sometime before the [[Clone Wars]], the building was purchased by the Wookiee Chalmun, who legitimized the business by turning it into a bar for spacers arriving into the busy spaceport of Mos Eisley. Over time, it became a successfull landmark of Tatooine, with many coming to the planet to pursue connections in the establishment. ===Clone Wars=== {{Quote|Leave your droids outside. You don't want Wuher on your case.''"
"''Keep your blaster holstered! Why ask for trouble?''"
"''Find an empty table or barstool. Crowding other customers will only end in tears.''"
"''Be friendly. Buy a drink for your neighbor at the bar.''"
"''Applaud the band—even if they're way off-key!''"
"''Make pleasant conversation and try to laugh at bad jokes.''"
"''Tip the bartender, especially if you make a mess.|Mos Eisley Cantina house rules|''[[Star Wars: Absolutely Everything You Need to Know]]''}} [[File:GunfightAtChalmunsCantina-SoI.png|thumb|300px|A firefight broke out between the Papanoidas and Greedo's cohorts.|left]] During the [[Blockade of Pantora]], the seedy bar, at this point run by a [[Nimbanel]] bartender, was a known haven for criminals and lowlifes, many under the employ of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure|Jabba]] the [[Hutt]]. [[Greedo]], a bounty hunter hired by [[Trade Federation]] official [[Sib Canay]], kidnapped [[Che Amanwe Papanoida]], the daughter of [[Pantoran Assembly|Pantoran]] [[Supreme Chairman]] [[Notluwiski Papanoida]], and held her captive in one of the cantina's back rooms. When Greedo was forced by Jabba to lead the Chairman Papanoida and his son [[Ion Papanoida|Ion]] to the cantina, a firefight broke out in the bar between the three [[Pantoran]]s and Greedo's associates.{{TCW|Sphere of Influence}} Around the time of the [[Battle of Kadavo]] during the [[Clone Wars]], two Alderaanian tourists visiting [[Tatooine]] died in a [[speeder bike]] accident. The ''[[Zygerrian News]]'' implied that the two had gotten intoxicated in the cantina of [[Mos Eisley]], thereby causing the accident.{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/escape-from-kadavo-trivia-gallery|text="Escape from Kadavo" Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 5}} [[File:Krayts_Claw_SaV.png|thumb|right|350px|The Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina was a regular hangout for the Krayt's Claw bounty hunters.]] Following the [[Battle of Dathomir]], [[Asajj Ventress]] made her way to the Mos Eisley cantina, where she began to drown her sorrows with [[prow]]. One of the patrons, a bounty hunter named [[Oked]], made a pass at her, and when he refused to relent, was killed by Ventress. Two other bounty hunters, [[Bossk'wassak'Cradossk|Bossk]] and [[Latts Razzi]], coerced Ventress into joining [[Safe cargo objective|their job]] in Oked's place.{{TCW|Bounty (episode)}} Soon after, Ventress and Razzi were in the cantina when Ventress felt a [[disturbance in the Force]], that disturbance being [[Savage Opress]] coming closer to locating his brother, [[Maul]].{{TCW|Brothers (episode)}} Once Opress had found Maul, the two began a [[Massacre on Raydonia|killing spree]] in order to draw out Maul's nemesis [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. Opress appeared on a screen displaying open bounties, prompting Ventress to take the job in an [[Skirmish above Raydonia|attempt]] to take down her former underling.{{TCW|Revenge (episode)}} Following the [[Mission to Raxus Secundus|mission]] to [[Raxus Secundus]], Ventress returned to Tatooine to enlist [[Boba Fett]]'s [[Krayt's Claw|Syndicate]] to help her rescue [[Jedi Master]] [[Quinlan Vos]] from [[Serenno]]. She met with [[Boba Fett]] in the cantina, and after some debating, the two [[Mission to Serenno (Krayt's Claw)|joined forces]] to free Vos.''[[Dark Disciple]]'' ===Galactic Civil War=== {{Quote|Sorry about the mess.|Han Solo tips after killing Greedo|}} [[File:BoShek-PotF.png|thumb|left|250px|BoShek was a regular of Chalmun's Cantina who introduced Ben Kenobi to Chewbacca.]] By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War]], the cantina had changed ownership twice since the Clone Wars: the [[Ranat]] siblings known as the [[Vriichon brothers]] turned the building into an illegal [[spice]] den, until the [[Wookiee]] [[Chalmun]] turned it back into a bar. Under his employ was the gruff human [[Wuher]], who, despite not being the friendliest bartender in the galaxy, was respected by his customers for never asking any questions, and [[Ackmena]], the [[Gender|female]] night-shift bartender, who had friendlier manners.''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary, New Edition]]'' Kenobi and [[Luke Skywalker]] came to the cantina shortly before the [[Destruction of Alderaan|destruction]] of [[Alderaan]] to charter a course to the doomed planet, in order to deliver [[Death Star plans|technical readouts]] on the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]]. After having their droids tossed out due to the cantina's no-droid policy, they hired the smuggler [[Han Solo]] and his compatriot [[Chewbacca]], who needed a way to pay off their debt to Jabba the Hutt. Their visit was not uneventful; the Jedi Master and his apprentice were harassed and confronted by [[Doctor]] [[Cornelius Evazan]] and his thug [[Ponda Baba]], the latter of whom lost his arm after provoking Kenobi. Afterwards, Solo had an altercation with the [[bounty hunter]] Greedo, who was eager to collect the bounty on Solo. Solo, however, killed him with a single shot after Greedo foolishly fired first—and missed. Han then tossed Wuher some credits and left the cantina to prep the ''[[Millennium Falcon]]'' for the two fugitives. [[File:Rogue_One_1_Star_Wars_40th.png|right|thumb|200px|Figrin D'an and the Modal Nodes performing at Chalmun's Cantina]] Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin]], [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] employed Fett to track down and capture the mysterious pilot who destroyed the Death Star. Knowing that Solo frequented Mos Eisley, the bounty hunter would interrogate and promptly murder his victims after they failed to provide any sufficient leads. Entering the Mos Eisley Cantina, Boba Fett addressed the gathered crowd that he would not leave until he was provided the information he sought. After brutally wounding a [[Unidentified six-armed alien|six-armed alien]] and intimidating the bar patrons, Wuher responded that no one knew the boy, and that he had only come in some time ago with Kenobi. After shooting a length of fibercord and causing a [[Unidentified moisture farmer (Chalmun's Cantina)|fleeing patron]] to fall down, the teen soon broke down and told Boba the boy's name—Luke Skywalker. He then shot the boy and headed towards [[Ben Kenobi's home]] in order to discover more about Luke's identity.[[Star Wars (2015) 5|''Star Wars'' (2015) 5]] Unbeknownst to Boba, his discovery of Skywalker's name at Chalmun's Cantina would soon allow Darth Vader to realize that he had a son.[[Darth Vader (2015) 6|''Darth Vader'' (2015) 6]] By [[3 ABY]],{{OutlawsDate}} it was taboo to sit at the booth where Han Solo shot Greedo. A superstition arose that everyone who sat at that booth got shot. Later [[Kay Vess]] came to meet [[Hoss]] where he gave [[Nix]] away to be sold to [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]. ===New Republic Era=== {{Quote|Hey, droid, I'm a hunter. I'm lookin' for some work.''"
"''Unfortunately, the Bounty Guild no longer operates from Tatooine.''"
"''I'm not looking for Guild work.''"
"''I am afraid that does not improve your situation, at least by my calculation.|Din Djarin and EV-9D9|audio=EVDroidandMandalorian-TMS01CH05.flac|{{TheMandalorian|Chapter 5: The Gunslinger}}}} [[File:Djarin-EVDroid-TMS1C5.jpg|thumb|left|350px|Din Djarin asks EV-9D9 about available jobs on Tatooine.]]By [[9 ABY]],{{MandoDate}} after the [[Battle of Jakku|fall]] of the Empire, the cantina was under a [[Unidentified boss|new boss]], and the no-droid policy was abolished. Multiple [[EV-series supervisor droid|supervisor droids]] served as the new bartenders. When [[bounty hunter]] [[Din Djarin]] visited the cantina looking for work, one of the droid bartenders, [[EV-9D9]],{{DisneyGallery|Connections}} stated that the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild]] no longer operated from Tatooine. He clarified that he operated separately from the Guild, but the droid replied stating that no work was available. Djarin was immediately greeted by rookie bounty hunter [[Toro Calican]], who teamed up with him to hunt down the [[assassin]] [[Fennec Shand]]. Djarin meet [[Peli Motto]] playing a game of [[sabacc]] with [[Doctor Mandible]]. Djarin agrees to meet Motto’s contact for a job.{{TheMandalorian|Chapter 10: The Passenger}} ==Behind the scenes== Crew members playing Bith musicians in the cantina moved to the beat of Benny Goodman's 1937 hit song "[[Wikipedia:Sing, Sing, Sing (With a Swing)|Sing, Sing, Sing]]" while filming on set. If one looks closely at the bar behind Wuher, one can see surplus jet engine parts that were later used for the head of bounty hunter droid [[IG-88B|IG-88]]. Filming for the cantina exteriors in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] took place in [[Ajim]], Djerba, [[Tunisia]] on [[April 2]]–[[April 3|3]], [[1976]]. However, most of the interior shots were from Stage 6 at [[Elstree Studios]] on [[April 13]]–[[April 21|21]]. For the background alien babble of the ''A New Hope'' scene, sound technician [[Ben Burtt]] had some crew members inhale helium and recorded them chatting, although it took four takes until they were able to get over the laughter and be able to converse; the oxygen deprivation also caused a tipsy effect in their session. The females ([[Lucy Autrey Wilson|Lucy Wilson]] and [[Bunny Alsup]]) were recorded separately and later their voices were slowed down; the opposite was made with the males ([[Colin Kitchens|Michael Kitchens]], [[Howie Hammerman]], [[Todd Boekelheide]]) and then they were mixed to create some of the background soundscape. More specific effects included the "laughter" of a hippopotamus and a spring peeper frog; a friend of Burtt's also was recorded reading Latin, and his voice was chopped in a synthesizer.[[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']] Chalmun's Cantina was the same cantina in the now non-[[canon]]ical ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'', set months after ''A New Hope'', and shown during a night shift. A skit on a [[1977]] episode of [[Wikipedia:The Richard Pryor Show|''The Richard Pryor Show'']] took place in the "Star Wars Bar," with Pryor playing the bartender. Though the skit did not reuse the original sets, many of the masks from ''A New Hope''{{'s}} cantina scene were used for the patrons. The cantina was featured in an official [[1979]] drunk-driving PSA. In 2007, [[StarWars.com|the official site]] [http://www.starwars.com/episode-iv/bts/article/cantinapsa.html made it available] for viewing. ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox| *''[[Star Wars Battlefront
